title: Cross-drawing intent envelope
description: Metaschema for cross-drawing intent payloads. When a skill declares `produces_intent: "<skill-id>"` it commits to emitting a stable subset of its IR conforming to a per-skill `<skill>-intent.schema.json` that is itself wrapped in this envelope. Consumer skills read these intents from sibling chats via the runtime's `cross_drawing_context` injection.

intent_version.description: Semver for the intent payload schema (independent of skill version). Bump major when removing/renaming a required field; minor when adding optional fields; patch for clarifications. Consumers dispatch on this version.

skill_version.description: Source skill version that produced this intent

chat_id.description: Optional chat/session id where this intent was produced

produced_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this intent payload was finalised

payload.description: The intent-specific data conforming to the producer skill's <skill>-intent.schema.json. Shape varies per intent_type.

$design_note: Why a separate intent vs full IR? The full IR includes positions, drawings, presentation data — downstream skills don't need it. The intent is a stability contract; the full IR can change in non-breaking ways without breaking downstream consumers. Forward-compatibility rule: new optional fields can be added freely; required fields cannot be removed or renamed without a major version bump.
